function solve(s) {
    const n = s.length;
    // We iterate i starting from 0. The maximum useful i is when i >= n - i - 1 is false, 
    // meaning 2*i <= n - 1, or i <= (n - 1) / 2.
    const limit = Math.floor((n - 1) / 2);
    
    for (let i = 0; i <= limit; i++) {
        const j = n - 1 - i;
        if (s[i] === s[j]) {
            // Since we iterate in increasing order of i, the first match found is the minimum index.
            return i;
        }
    }
    
    // If the loop completes, no such index was found.
    return -1;
}
